status: P0 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 1 / CRITICAL PRODUCT IDENTITY BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Chemically under-specified erythropoiesis-stimulating glycoprotein biologic family
mechanism_summary: Erythropoietin receptor agonism stimulating erythroid progenitor survival, differentiation and red-blood-cell production.
evidence_maturity: Extensive authorised anaemia-treatment evidence for defined epoetin and related products; catalogue identity is insufficiently specified.
regulatory_headline: AUTHORISED ESA MEDICINES EXIST / CATALOGUE PRODUCT IDENTITY UNRESOLVED / ATHLETE PROHIBITION
certification_blockers: Exact molecule: epoetin alfa, beta, theta, zeta, darbepoetin or other
Manufacturer, cell line, glycosylation and formulation
Indication-specific haemoglobin target and risk controls
closure_class: CRITICAL GLYCOPROTEIN IDENTITY, POTENCY AND THROMBOSIS
closure_state: BLOCKED — ESA MOLECULE, GLYCOSYLATION, CELL LINE AND PRODUCT UNKNOWN
authoritative_product_boundary: Authorised erythropoiesis-stimulating agents are exact glycoprotein biologics with product-specific indications, potency units, manufacturing and boxed safety controls. The catalogue EPO vial cannot inherit epoetin-alfa or other ESA evidence.
critical_closure_controls: Death, myocardial infarction, stroke and thrombosis risks retained
Hypertension, seizure and tumour-progression contexts retained
